rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Release Of Property From Lien And For Judgment That No Lien Exists, And For Costs And Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 1. PETITIONER PARAMOUNT ESTATE LLC Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Release Of Property From Lien And For Judgment That No Lien Exists, And For Costs And Attorneys Fees is GRANTED. The subject property is ordered released from the claim of lien. (Code Civ. Proc., 8488(b).) Petitioner is awarded $4,005 in attorney's fees. (Code Civ. Proc., 8488(c).) No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HELENA DALESSIO ET AL VS. THOMAS PAINE SQUARE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Shelby A. Kennick
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT THOMAS PAINE SQUARE, LLC, DOMUS MANAGEMENT Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint is moot in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is moot regarding the request for attorney's fees in light of ruling on the demurrer. Motion is granted as to the request for treble damages, for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, to support each element of CC 3345. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: HELENA DALESSIO ET AL VS. THOMAS PAINE SQUARE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT THOMAS PAINE SQUARE, LLC, DOMUS MANAGEMENT DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts giving rise to violation of CC 1942.5 and Financial Elder Abuse cause of action. The Court notes that no cases with similar facts were cited in opposition to the demurrer. The only cited case (Mahan v. Charles W. Chan Ins. Agency, Inc., 14 Cal.App.5th 841) is factually distinguishable and Plaintiff's complaint does not contain facts that would bring their claims within the reasoning of Mahan used to find applicability of the Elder Abuse statute to the property rights of the aggrieved party. Furthermore, if Plaintiff's Elder Abuse claim is based in fraud, all facts must be stated with requisite specificity. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: AUSTIN RAYMOND FARLEY VS. JOHN D. MORIARTY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Moriartys Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 6. DEFENDANT JOHN MORIARTY Notice Of Defendant Moriartys Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7 is DENIED. The first amended complaint was not filed for an improper purpose and is not indisputably without merit. (Code Civ. Proc.,128.7.) The Court cannot resolve disputed factual issues at this time.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: XIAOQI WU VS. GINA FINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 7. PLAINTIFF XIAOQI WU MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that it is entitled to a judgment on all the causes of action alleged (e,g. evidence to support each element of forcible entry). No motion for Summary Adjudication properly noticed (See Notice of Motion) or addressed in the Points and Authorities. Furthermore, triable issues of fact exist as to defendant's possession of the unit subject to this action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: GREG WILLIAMS VS. LA PERLA NORTH AMERICA, INC., A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 10. DEFENDANT LA PERLA NORTH AMERICA, INC. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. The Complaint is uncertain as to the issue of possession. (Code Civ. Proc.,430.10 subd. (f).) The unlawful detainer seeks to recover possession, however the Complaint states that Defendant has abandoned the premises. (Complaint, 13.) Request for Judicial notice re: letters is denied.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ALTON MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. MARK JORDAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 27, 2023 line 11. DEFENDANT MARK JORDAN DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. The Complaint is uncertain as to whether a tenancy exists. (Code Civ. Proc.,430.10 subd. (f).) The Complaint states both that Defendant is an unapproved holdover tenant (60-Day Notice to Terminate.) and that the parties entered into a written lease. (Complaint, 6). No opposition filed.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
